metrics.name: Banxico
metrics.question: Will the Banxico Governing Board's June 25, 2026, interest rate decision be unanimous (5-0 vote)?

reversal_search.why: every actor already holds a reversal-capable state

simulation_result.question: Will the Banxico Governing Board's June 25, 2026, interest rate decision be unanimous (5-0 vote)?
limitations: forecast decomposition — grounded prior 0.8333 (counted n=2); simulation-conditional 0.0988 (resolved mass 1.00); headline 0.0988 via combiner_unavailable_range_only. Prior and simulation are reported separately and never blended by a fixed rule.
bounded omitted-state residual 0.143: the interval widens to [0.0, 0.5236] — private-state omissions are BOUNDS, never unknown-state worlds

no leakage-audited prior↔simulation reliability combiner is fitted — the headline is the mass-based recovery blend (resolved mass keeps its simulated conditional; unresolved mass takes the grounded prior); the two inputs are reported separately and never blended by a fixed rule

blueprint.causal_thesis: The May 7, 2026, 3-2 split vote to cut rates to 6.50% revealed deep internal disagreement, with Heath and Espinosa preferring a hold. The declared end of the easing cycle sets the stage for a hold decision in June, but the dissenters may still oppose further cuts while the majority may push for more

terminal_canonicalization.why: vote terminals read the tally directly

easing_cycle_ended.claim: Banxico declared its two-year easing cycle over at the May 7, 2026 meeting, with a split vote.

provenance.definition: Banxico declared its two-year easing cycle over at the May 7, 2026 meeting, with a split vote.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Banxico ends easing cycle with 3-2 cut to 6.50% on May 7, 2026.
cases.basis_quote: The central bank declared an end to its two-year easing cycle.

internal_disagreement_persists.claim: The 3-2 split at the May 2026 meeting indicates meaningful internal disagreement on the board.

provenance.definition: The 3-2 split at the May 2026 meeting indicates meaningful internal disagreement on the board.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: May 7, 2026 Banxico vote was 3-2, with Heath and Espinosa dissenting.
cases.basis_quote: Board members Jonathan Heath and Irene Espinosa Cantellano dissented, preferring to hold the rate at 6.75%.

economic_contraction_and_inflation_forec.claim: Mexico's economy contracted and inflation forecast for Q2 2026 was revised upward to 4.1%.

provenance.definition: Mexico's economy contracted and inflation forecast for Q2 2026 was revised upward to 4.1%.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Banxico cited contraction in economic activity and upward revision of inflation forecast.
cases.basis_quote: The central bank cited a contraction in economic activity and revised its inflation forecast for Q2 2026 upward to 4.1%.

provenance.definition: All five members of the Governing Board vote for the same policy action (hold, cut, or hike).
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Any dissenting vote (4-1, 3-2, etc.) or meeting cancellation/postponement beyond July 1, 2026.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Banxico's previous unanimous votes before the split in 2026.
cases.basis_quote: The Governing Board unanimously decided to cut the target for the overnight interbank funding rate by 25 basis points.
The Governing Board unanimously decided to maintain the target for the overnight interbank funding rate.

banxico_gb.quorum: At least three members must be present to vote.
banxico_gb.consequence_of_nonparticipation: If a member is absent without delegation, the vote proceeds with remaining members; if quorum is not met, the meeting is rescheduled.

weight_invariant.rule: every weight traces to a counted reference class or is explicit unknown mass; no qualitative label is mapped to a number

reversal_search.why: every actor already holds a reversal-capable state

victoria_rodriguez_ceja.residual_provenance: decision-spanning basis: every feasible option has a represented state

jonathan_heath.residual_provenance: uncovered feasible option(s) ['hike'] with no counted cases — bounded per option

irene_espinosa_cantellano.residual_provenance: decision-spanning basis: every feasible option has a represented state

galia_borja_gomez.residual_provenance: uncovered feasible option(s) ['hike'] with no counted cases — bounded per option

omar_mejia_castelazo.residual_provenance: uncovered feasible option(s) ['hike'] with no counted cases — bounded per option

victoria_rodriguez_ceja.claim: Victoria believes that despite the economic contraction, the inflation forecast remains dangerously sticky due to persistent supply-side pressures and peso depreciation risks. She sees any rate cut as premature and likely to entrench inflation expectations above target, undermining Banxico's credibi
beliefs: Inflation is not yet sustainably converging to the 3% target
A cut now would signal panic and weaken the peso further
The contraction is temporary and supply-driven, not demand-deficient
goals: Anchor inflation expectations firmly
Preserve Banxico's hard-won credibility
Avoid repeating the 2021-2022 policy mistake of cutting too early
victoria_rodriguez_ceja.pressures: Internal disagreement persists; some board members favor a cut to support growth, but Victoria sees that as a threat to price stability.
stances: Skeptical of easing cycle ending as a reason to cut
historical_case_refs: Banxico's 2021 hold-while-contracting stance under Governor Díaz de León
distinguishing_observations: Victoria has publicly emphasized inflation risks in recent speeches
She voted against the majority in a prior meeting (hypothetical but consistent with her known hawkishness)
victoria_rodriguez_ceja.action_if_state: Vote to hold the rate, dissenting if the majority favors a cut, thereby breaking unanimity.

victoria_rodriguez_ceja.claim: Victoria privately concludes that the economic contraction is severe and demand-driven, and that the inflation forecast is largely transitory. She believes the easing cycle has indeed ended, but that a further cut is necessary to prevent a deeper recession and deflation spiral. She will vote for a c
beliefs: The contraction is more serious than official forecasts suggest
Inflation will fall quickly as demand collapses
Banxico must act decisively to support growth and avoid a hard landing
goals: Stimulate economic activity
Prevent deflation risk
Align with global central bank easing trends
victoria_rodriguez_ceja.pressures: Internal disagreement persists; some board members want to hold, but Victoria feels the cost of inaction is too high.
stances: Willing to accept temporary inflation overshoot
historical_case_refs: Banxico's 2020 emergency cuts during the COVID-19 recession
distinguishing_observations: Victoria has recently met with business leaders warning of layoffs
She has privately expressed concern about the peso's overvaluation hurting exports
victoria_rodriguez_ceja.action_if_state: Vote to cut the rate, dissenting if the majority favors a hold, thereby breaking unanimity.

victoria_rodriguez_ceja.claim: Victoria believes that any dissent, regardless of direction, would signal deep division and undermine Banxico's credibility at a critical juncture. She prioritizes presenting a united front to markets, even if it means voting against her own preferred policy. She will vote with the majority to maint
beliefs: Market confidence is more fragile than inflation or growth data suggest
A split vote would trigger capital flight and peso depreciation
Unanimity itself is a policy tool to anchor expectations
goals: Maintain Banxico's institutional reputation
Avoid financial instability
Signal predictability to investors
victoria_rodriguez_ceja.pressures: Internal disagreement persists, but Victoria sees the external cost of division as outweighing any policy difference.
historical_case_refs: Banxico's 2023 unanimous hold despite internal splits (speculative but plausible)
distinguishing_observations: Victoria has emphasized 'institutional unity' in recent off-the-record comments
She is aware of past episodes where dissent led to market turmoil (e.g., 2016)
victoria_rodriguez_ceja.action_if_state: Vote with the majority (whether hold, cut, or hike) to achieve a 5-0 outcome, suppressing her own preference if necessary.

jonathan_heath.claim: Jonathan Heath privately believes that despite the economic contraction, inflation expectations are becoming entrenched above target, so the only responsible action is to hold rates steady and signal a prolonged pause, even if it means overriding colleagues who favor a cut.
beliefs: Inflation expectations are de-anchoring and a cut would worsen them.
The easing cycle has ended prematurely and further easing is too risky.
goals: Prevent inflation from becoming entrenched above the 3% target.
Maintain Banxico's credibility as an inflation fighter.
jonathan_heath.pressures: Internal pressure from dovish board members to cut, but Heath feels the institutional mandate for price stability outweighs growth concerns.
historical_case_refs: Heath's past dissents against rate cuts in 2020-2021 when inflation was rising.
distinguishing_observations: Heath has previously voted against the majority when inflation risks were elevated.
He often emphasizes forward guidance and inflation expectations in his public statements.
jonathan_heath.action_if_state: Vote to hold rates and argue forcefully for a hold in the meeting, potentially dissenting if the majority favors a cut.

jonathan_heath.claim: Jonathan Heath privately believes that the economic contraction is severe and that inflation is temporary, so a rate cut is necessary to avoid a deep recession, and he is willing to break with his usual hawkish stance.
beliefs: The contraction is more serious than the inflation risk.
Inflation will subside on its own as demand falls.
goals: Stimulate economic activity to prevent a prolonged downturn.
Align with the government's growth agenda.
jonathan_heath.pressures: Strong political and public pressure to support growth, and internal pressure from dovish members.
historical_case_refs: Heath voted for a cut in 2019 when growth slowed sharply, despite inflation being above target.
distinguishing_observations: Heath has shown flexibility in the past when growth risks are acute.
He may prioritize employment over inflation in extreme downturns.
jonathan_heath.action_if_state: Vote to cut rates and try to persuade other hawks that the contraction justifies a bold move.

jonathan_heath.claim: Jonathan Heath privately believes that a unanimous decision is more important than the exact policy direction, so he will vote to hold even if he personally prefers a cut, in order to present a united front and avoid signaling internal disagreement.
beliefs: A split vote would damage Banxico's credibility more than a suboptimal rate.
The market values unanimity as a sign of institutional strength.
goals: Preserve the appearance of board cohesion.
Avoid market volatility from a dissent.
jonathan_heath.pressures: Pressure from the governor to deliver a unanimous decision, and awareness that a split could be exploited politically.
historical_case_refs: Heath has previously voted with the majority to maintain unanimity, even when he had reservations.
distinguishing_observations: Heath has emphasized the importance of clear communication and unity in past statements.
He may view the vote as a signal to markets rather than a pure policy choice.
jonathan_heath.action_if_state: Vote to hold, even if his personal preference is different, to ensure a 5-0 outcome.

irene_espinosa_cantellano.claim: Irene privately believes that despite the easing cycle having ended and economic contraction, inflation forecasts remain dangerously sticky due to supply-side shocks and peso depreciation. She sees a hold as the only way to avoid fueling stagflation, and she will insist on holding rates steady even
historical_case_refs: Banxico 2021 hold during contraction to anchor inflation expectations
irene_espinosa_cantellano.action_if_state: Vote to hold the policy rate, and argue forcefully for unanimity on hold to signal resolve against inflation.

irene_espinosa_cantellano.claim: Irene privately believes that the economic contraction is severe and that inflation is forecast to fall rapidly as demand collapses. She thinks a cut is necessary to prevent a deep recession and that the easing cycle should resume. She will push for a cut and may dissent if others hold or hike.
historical_case_refs: Banxico 2020 emergency cuts during pandemic contraction
irene_espinosa_cantellano.action_if_state: Vote to cut the policy rate, and if necessary, dissent to force a split if the majority favors hold or hike.

irene_espinosa_cantellano.claim: Irene privately believes that internal disagreement is damaging Banxico's credibility and that the exact policy direction matters less than presenting a united front. She will subordinate her personal view to whatever achieves a 5-0 vote, whether hold, cut, or hike, to restore institutional reputati
historical_case_refs: Banxico 2024 unanimous hold despite internal splits in minutes
irene_espinosa_cantellano.action_if_state: Vote with the majority to ensure a 5-0 outcome, regardless of her own preferred policy.

galia_borja_gomez.claim: Galia Borja Gómez privately believes that the easing cycle has ended prematurely and that the economic contraction combined with persistent inflation forecasts requires a hawkish hold to prevent further inflationary expectations from becoming entrenched. She sees internal disagreement as a risk to B
beliefs: The easing cycle is over and any further cut would fuel inflation.
A hold is the only responsible action given the inflation forecast.
Unanimity reinforces Banxico's independence and credibility.
goals: Maintain price stability as the primary mandate.
Avoid a split vote that could undermine market confidence.
Signal a cautious, data-dependent approach.
galia_borja_gomez.pressures: Institutional pressure to present a united front despite internal disagreement.
stances: Favors holding rates at current level.
historical_case_refs: Banxico 2021 hold during inflation spike despite growth slowdown
distinguishing_observations: She has previously emphasized inflation targeting over growth support.
galia_borja_gomez.action_if_state: Vote to hold the policy rate and advocate for a unanimous statement emphasizing inflation vigilance.

galia_borja_gomez.claim: Galia Borja Gómez privately believes that the economic contraction is severe enough to warrant a rate cut despite inflation concerns, and that the easing cycle should continue. She views the inflation forecast as transitory and driven by supply-side factors, and thinks a cut now can support growth w
beliefs: The contraction is the greater risk than inflation.
Inflation is temporary and supply-driven.
A cut now can boost growth without causing persistent inflation.
goals: Support economic activity and employment.
Continue the easing cycle to avoid recession.
Achieve a unanimous decision to show board alignment.
galia_borja_gomez.pressures: Institutional pressure to act decisively and unanimously to support the economy.
stances: Believes inflation will moderate on its own.
historical_case_refs: Banxico 2020 emergency cuts during pandemic recession
distinguishing_observations: She has previously supported counter-cyclical measures.
galia_borja_gomez.action_if_state: Vote to cut the policy rate and push for a unanimous statement highlighting support for growth.

galia_borja_gomez.claim: Galia Borja Gómez privately believes that the internal disagreement is so deep that any rate change would fracture the board, damaging credibility. She prefers a hold as a compromise that allows a unanimous vote, even though she personally might lean toward a cut or hike. Her priority is to avoid a
beliefs: A split vote would be worse than any policy error.
The economic outlook is too uncertain to justify a decisive move.
A hold is the safest option to maintain unity.
goals: Preserve board cohesion and institutional reputation.
Avoid a dissenting vote at all costs.
Delay a contentious decision until more clarity emerges.
galia_borja_gomez.pressures: Intense institutional pressure to avoid a public split.
stances: Neutral on rate direction in the short term.
historical_case_refs: Banxico 2022 hold amid board divisions over inflation vs growth
distinguishing_observations: She has acted as a bridge between hawkish and dovish members in past meetings.
galia_borja_gomez.action_if_state: Vote to hold and actively work to persuade other members to join a unanimous hold, emphasizing the need for unity.

omar_mejia_castelazo.claim: Omar believes the easing cycle has ended prematurely and that the current economic contraction is largely supply-side, not demand-side. He sees the inflation forecast as still above target due to persistent cost-push pressures, and thinks a hold is necessary to signal Banxico's commitment to price s
beliefs: The easing cycle ended too soon; inflation is not yet vanquished.
Economic contraction is driven by supply shocks, not weak demand, so rate cuts would not help.
A split vote would undermine Banxico's inflation-fighting credibility.
goals: Restore and maintain Banxico's hawkish credibility.
Prevent any rate cut that could reignite inflation expectations.
Achieve a unanimous vote to show board cohesion.
omar_mejia_castelazo.pressures: Internal pressure from colleagues who may favor a cut due to contraction; external pressure from markets expecting a dovish pivot.
stances: Opposes any cut, even if growth weakens further.
Willing to dissent if others vote to cut, but prefers unanimity on hold.
historical_case_refs: Banxico's 2021-2023 tightening cycle where they hiked aggressively to curb inflation, showing institutional hawkishness.
distinguishing_observations: Omar has previously emphasized inflation risks in public statements.
He may reference the 2022-2023 period when Banxico held rates despite economic slowdown.
omar_mejia_castelazo.action_if_state: Vote to hold the policy rate and actively lobby other board members to vote hold, threatening to dissent if a cut is proposed, but ultimately seeking a unanimous hold.

omar_mejia_castelazo.claim: Omar believes the easing cycle has ended only in name, but the economic contraction is severe and demand-driven, with inflation forecast falling below target. He thinks Banxico must cut rates preemptively to avoid a deeper recession, and that internal disagreement reflects genuine differences in out
beliefs: The easing cycle should continue; the contraction is demand-side and requires stimulus.
Inflation is forecast to fall below target, so rate cuts are safe.
Internal disagreement is healthy and should be reflected in the vote.
goals: Stimulate economic growth and avoid recession.
Prevent deflationary spiral.
Vote according to his own analysis, even if it means a split.
omar_mejia_castelazo.pressures: Pressure from business groups and government officials to ease policy; counter-pressure from conservative board members.
stances: Believes holding would be a policy mistake.
Accepts that a split vote is better than a wrong unanimous decision.
historical_case_refs: Banxico's 2019-2020 easing cycle when they cut rates during a slowdown, showing precedent for dovish action.
distinguishing_observations: Omar may have recently made statements about downside risks to growth.
He could cite falling core inflation as evidence that policy can loosen.
omar_mejia_castelazo.action_if_state: Vote to cut the policy rate and publicly argue for the cut, accepting a 4-1 or 3-2 split if others disagree.

omar_mejia_castelazo.claim: Omar is primarily focused on the institutional incentive for a unanimous vote, regardless of the specific policy action. He believes that a split vote would signal disarray and harm Banxico's credibility more than any single rate decision. He is willing to compromise on the direction (hold, cut, or
beliefs: Unanimity is paramount for market confidence and institutional reputation.
The specific rate decision matters less than the appearance of board cohesion.
Internal disagreement can be managed through compromise and negotiation.
goals: Achieve a 5-0 vote at all costs.
Maintain Banxico's credibility as a unified institution.
Avoid being the dissenter or having any dissenter.
omar_mejia_castelazo.pressures: Strong institutional pressure to present a united front; personal desire to avoid being seen as a source of division.
stances: Flexible on the policy action; prioritizes process over outcome.
Will support hold, cut, or hike if it secures unanimity.
Opposes any vote that results in a split.
historical_case_refs: Banxico's historical tendency to deliver unanimous decisions, especially during uncertain times (e.g., 2020 pandemic mee
distinguishing_observations: Omar may have privately expressed concern about the optics of a split vote.
He could be the key swing vote that brokers a compromise.
omar_mejia_castelazo.action_if_state: Engage in behind-the-scenes negotiations to find a policy action that all five members can agree on, and then vote accordingly, even if it means abandoning his initial preferred stance.

deliberations.note: I have already voted to hold the rate, prioritizing unanimity. The internal conflict is unresolved, but I lack precise knowledge of other members' preferences. Gathering information on their leanings

coalescer.randomness_rule: content-addressed (state/context-seeded) draws — equal keys share the future transition law by construction; independent streams carry a tag that refuses the merge

engine_primary.weight_source: counted_reference_class_posteriors (no qualitative label is mapped to a number anywhere in this engine); represented states carry the full branch mass — omitted-state residuals are bounded interval-wideners, never unknown-state worlds

skipped_reasons: no deterministic trigger fired: primary stable, no verified evidence conflict, no reversal-capable disputed assumption (imagination alone is not a trigger)

replicate_policy.reason: behavioral replicates not requested (deterministic execution: content-addressed draws make replicate 0 exhaustive)

provenance.fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 2 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

notes: no leakage-audited reliability combiner is fitted — prior and simulation are reported separately with the feasible combined range; no fixed blend is applied and Lean V2 must not become default on this basis

consequences.boundary_contract: delivery mechanics precompiled; interpretation/persuasion/reaction remain branch-local actor decisions

record_vote.description: Each board member records their vote for the interest rate decision.

cancel_meeting.description: Banxico cancels or postpones the June 25 meeting beyond July 1.
